_comment: Entries the schema-drift guard should ignore. Each: {file (endsWith match), ref? (table.col; omit to match the whole file), reason}. Prefer FIXING drift over allowlisting; use this only for guard false-positives and explicitly-deferred work.

allow.reason: DEFERRED (legacy TM module): raw SQL queries the current `tasks` table using the pre-role-simplification schema (t.assignee_id, u.full_name, status 'delayed') which no longer exists. Fixing requires migrating/removing the whole legacy task-management module (owner_user_id, name, real status set), a refactor out of scope for a drift-repair run. Pre-existing breakage; see final report.
DEFERRED (legacy TM module): same superseded-schema raw SQL as tm/cron/reminders.
DEFERRED (legacy TM module): superseded-schema raw SQL + dead role values ('dg','agency_admin').

DEFERRED (legacy TM module): every query assumes the pre-simplification tasks schema (t.assignee_id, t.created_by, users.full_name). The module (app/api/tm/**) needs a migration or removal decision, not a column rename. Pre-existing breakage; see final report.

DEFERRED (unapplied migration): direct_outreach_cases/updates/sync_state/open_v are defined by supabase/migrations/144_direct_outreach.sql but that migration was never applied to prod (0 rows in information_schema). Code is correct; fix is to APPLY migration 144. Not auto-applied here because 144 includes ALTER ... ENABLE RLS + a seed INSERT (outside this run's CREATE-TABLE/ADD-COLUMN-only pre-approval). See final report.
DEFERRED (unapplied migration 144) — same as opdirect.ts; apply migration 144 to prod to enable.
